name: Trial activity policy systematization

scope.question: How should new trial launches and watched-trial milestones be systematized for /activity without admitting routine registry maintenance?

summary: Systematized trial activity policy. /trials remains the comprehensive surface for registry maintenance, stale checks, completion dates, result status, and no-results context. /activity is reserved for notable field movement: new directly relevant human trials, first-in-human or first older-adult milestones, material status changes, completions, terminations, posted results, publications, and major phase or clinical-program transitions. Added trial_activity_kind to live trial activity records and validation to reject routine_registry_maintenance from /activity. Reframed the sirolimus older-adults item as a 2022 direct older-adult rapalog trial-watch anchor rather than a February 2026 routine registry-update event.

materiality_decision.rationale: The change clarifies existing activity classification and keeps routine registry maintenance out of the public activity feed. No evidence interpretation changes.

search_log.summary: Reviewed all live trial-related activity items and assigned trial_activity_kind.
Used the local source published_on date to reframe the sirolimus record as a trial-launch/watch-anchor item rather than a routine registry update.

notes: Routine registry rechecks belong in trial_details or trial_watch_checks, not /activity.
Public trial activity items now validate only when trial_activity_kind is a public-threshold kind.
next_actions: When adding future trial activity, ask whether the event is a new direct trial, material milestone, result, completion, termination, or phase/program transition.
If the answer is only registry maintenance, update /trials metadata and research-session trial_watch_checks instead of creating an activity item.
